2025 Winners Gallery

Join the 8th edition of the Design Educates Awards 2026

for Meaningful Architecture and Design!




The Design Educates Awards (DEAwards) recognize exceptional
— Architecture
— Design for Impact
that are remarkable not only for their aesthetics, but also for their impact on society.




What We Are Looking For

We seek Architecture (1) that have the potential to make a meaningful

contribution to the world – at every scale, from the smallest shelter or

exhibition installation to residential, public, and urban-scale solutions.

In addition to realized projects, conceptual proposals are also accepted and

will be evaluated separately.

The special category, Design for Impact (2), includes non-architectural designs,

products, and concepts that create meaningful benefits for people, society, and

the environment.

We welcome participation from small to large firms, alongside emerging

designers and established practices from around the world. Experience shows

that among the winners, both groups are consistently represented.

The submitted materials should preferably highlight the project's added value,

articulating its significance beyond purely aesthetic qualities. Works must not

be older than five years.

Awards Brief

Winners Gallery

Our esteemed jury consists of world-renowned architects, designers, innovators and educators who are successfully expanding the boundaries of what is possible in their fields.

Check Jury members from previous editions here

Architecture & Design with Added Value

“Many architecture and design awards recognize and promote the “beauty” of successful design. But in the Design Educates Awards, we wanted to go one step further. We do not just want to see aesthetically and technically pleasing designs—we want something that offers true added value. We want an additional benefit with long-term implications that considers the increasing complexity of our lives, both now and in the future.“

Dr. Peter Kuczia
Curator & Initiator of the DEAwards

Competition Yearbook

More about the idea of the awards in our post-competition books.

Dates

Submissions open: April 01, 2026

Project submission deadline: June 15,2026

Jury deliberation: July 2026

Announcement of winners: August 2026

Awards Ceremony in Warsaw, Poland 2026: October 16, 2026

Fees

Students: 50 EUR
Individual professional / Company > 10 employees: 175 EUR
Company 11 – 100 employees: 350 EUR
Company 100+ employees: 650 EUR

*If a project is submitted to both categories, the fee for the second category is reduced by 50%

There are no further fees!

The fees support the organization of the competition and the statutory activities of the non-profit Laka Foundation (host of the DEAwards). Pariticiaption in the awards ceremony is free. All winners receive printed and framed certificates and books for free at the ceremony.

Winners Benefits

In each category:

(1) Architecture

(2) Design for Impact

following awards and recognition will be given:




Winner of the Year

Gold Prize

Silver Prize

Bronze Prize

Special Recognition

Honorable Mention




In addition to the general evaluation, the student project with the highest score

will be awarded the label: „Emerging Designer”.




Additionally:

1) Winners packages with certificates and winning labels
2) Inclusion in the yearbook (ISBN registered)
3) Participation in the exhibition with confirmation by certificates
4) Complimentary attendance at the Award Ceremony in Warsaw, Poland, with photo documentation
5) Press kits

6) Permanent listing in the Winners Gallery on www.designeducates.com

7) Promotion through media sponsors and partners

What to Submit

The submission consists of the following files:

1. Images of your design saved as JPG files in RGB mode with 200 dpi (up to 5 files),

including

2. A single featured image saved as JPG files in RGB mode with 200 dpi, in horizontal format, 2000 x 1000 px (this image will be used as a header image while presenting your design to the judges and media),

3. Description of your design (max. 1500 characters) and your team (max. 250 characters).

All materials should be uploaded via the User Panel.

规则.text:
中文

日本語

한국어

EN

Organizer

The host of the awards is Laka Foundation (KRS: 0000639902, EU VAT No. PL6381817438, Poland), a nonprofit and nongovernmental organization. Contact person: Damian Przybyła,  damian@designeducates.com, address: Stanisława Konarskiego 18c, 44-100 Gliwice, Poland.  The official website of the awards (www.designeducates.com) includes information about the theme, awards, jury, schedule, fees, submission requirements, and awards ceremony. Contact the organizer at hello@designeducates.com.

Eligibility

The awards are open to everyone. Participants can submit multiple designs in the same category, multiple designs in different categories, or a single design in more than one category, and in teams or individually. There is no limit as to the number of participants per team. Underage persons must apply through their legal guardians. Persons involved in the awards organization and jury (and their relatives and employees) cannot participate. Projects must be submitted in English. Projects submitted after the deadline for submissions will not be taken into account.

Fees and participation

Participation is equivalent to (1) acceptance of these guidelines, (2) declaring possession of copyright to the project, (3) consent to personal data processing for the purposes of the awards, and (4) consent to publish the entered project according to the license described below.  The registration fees are non-refundable (except fees transferred after the deadline for registration). Registration fees support the development of the awards.

Awards

Laureates of the main prizes and honorable mentions will receive: certificates and winning labels, inclusion in the book "Design Educates", invitation to participate in the Awards Ceremony and Exhibition in Germany.

Evaluation

On the awards’ website, the organizer includes information about the jury members and the evaluation criteria. The decision of the jury will be final. The winners will be announced on the website and notified via email.

License

Participants retain their full moral rights to the submitted projects and grant a free license to the organizer in order to allow the promotion of the results. The organizer is obliged to include the authors’ names each time it uses the license. The license has no time or territorial restrictions and can be used in the following fields: (1) public display, presentation, reproduction, and making the work available to the public; (2) making copies of the work (printing, reprographic, magnetic recording, digital techniques); and (3) using the work and its fragments for the awards’, participants’, and organizer’s promotion. The organizer cannot modify the submitted projects in any way without their authors’ consent.

Personal information

Participants’ personal information is processed according to the Privacy Policy. While publishing the results, the organizer will include the participants’ personal information: names, countries, and (if provided) information about their schools, companies, and professions. Any other personal information about participants will not be made available to any third party.
